---
title: "4.2.3 Translators"
subtitle: "Software P168-P169"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .figure-placeholder { border: 1px dashed #8aa0bf; border-radius: 8px; background: #f4f8ff; color: #40526b; padding: 1.1em 1.3em; margin: 0.8em 0; font-size: 0.9em; text-align: center; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.74em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.34em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 4 Directory / 返回 Unit 4 目录](../../index.html)

## Translators

### Why programs need translation

Computer programs are written by humans in a form trained programmers can understand.

To be used by a computer, programs need to be translated into binary instructions, or <span class="term">machine code</span>.

Humans find binary very difficult to read, but computers can only perform operations written in binary.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 4.15 Translation screenshot placeholder
:::

## Translator Programs

### Different translators for different tasks

A program must be translated into binary before a computer can use it.

This is done by a utility program called a <span class="term">translator</span> (翻译程序).

There are several types of translator program in use; each one performs a different task.

## Compilers

### Whole-program translation

A <span class="term">compiler</span> translates an entire high-level language program into machine code all in one go.

After compilation:

::: {.tight-list}
- the machine code can be directly used by the computer
- it can be used again and again without re-compilation
- if errors are detected, an error report is produced instead of a compiled program
:::

## Interpreters

### Statement-by-statement execution

An <span class="term">interpreter</span> reads a statement from a high-level language program, translates it and performs the specified action.

It then does the same with the next statement.

If there is an error, execution ceases and an error message is output, sometimes with a suggested correction.

A program needs to be interpreted again each time it is run.

## Assemblers

### Assembly language to machine code

An <span class="term">assembler</span> translates a program written in assembly language into machine code.

Once a program is assembled:

::: {.tight-list}
- the machine code can be directly used by the computer
- the same task can be performed without re-assembly
:::

## Table 4.3

### What each translator translates

::: {.clean-table}
| Compiler | Interpreter | Assembler |
|---|---|---|
| Translates a high-level language program into machine code | Executes a high-level language program one statement at a time | Translates a low-level assembly language program into machine code |
| Produces an executable file of machine code | Does not produce an executable file of machine code | Produces an executable file of machine code |
:::

## Table 4.3

### How translated programs are used

::: {.clean-table}
| Compiler | Interpreter | Assembler |
|---|---|---|
| Compiled programs are run without the compiler | Interpreted programs cannot be run without the interpreter | Assembled programs are used without the assembler |
| A compiled program is usually distributed for general use | An interpreter is often used when a program is being developed | An assembled program is usually distributed for general use |
:::

## Classroom Check

### Compare translator programs

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- a translator converts a program into machine code
- a compiler translates the whole high-level program at once
- an interpreter translates and executes one statement at a time
- an assembler translates assembly language into machine code
- compiler and assembler output can be used without re-translation
- interpreted programs need the interpreter each time they run
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Compilers and interpreters

The next deck compares the advantages and disadvantages of compilers and interpreters.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 4 Directory / 返回 Unit 4 目录](../../index.html)
